SEARCH
Package view

Class MonsterView

java.lang.Object
view.MonsterView
public class MonsterView extends Object
Class to manage the position of the monsters for the view package so that the package does not need to communicate with the model
Author:
Scarselli Ilaria, matricola 1918975

  • Constructor Details

    • MonsterView

      public MonsterView(String name, int x, int y)
      Class constructor
      Parameters:
      name - the type of the monster
      x - the x-coordinate of the monster in pixels
      y - the y-coordinate of the monster in pixels

  • Method Details

    • getMonsterImage

      public void getMonsterImage(String imageName)
      Assigns the images to draw the animation for the monsters
      Parameters:
      imageName - the type of the monster

    • setup

      public BufferedImage setup(String imageName)
      Method to retrieve the image from the resource folder
      Parameters:
      imageName - the name of the image to substitute in the path for the resource folder
      Returns:
      the image

    • getViewX

      public int getViewX()
      Gets the x-coordinate for the view package to draw the monster
      Returns:
      the x-coordinate in pixels

    • setViewX

      public void setViewX(int x)
      Sets the x-coordinate to draw the monster on screen
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate in pixels

    • getViewY

      public int getViewY()
      Gets the y-coordinate to draw the monster on screen
      Returns:
      the y-coordinate in pixels

    • setViewY

      public void setViewY(int y)
      Sets the y-coordinate to draw the monster on screen
      Parameters:
      y - the y-coordinate in pixels

    • getViewDirection

      public String getViewDirection()
      Gets the direction of the monster
      Returns:
      the direction of the monster

    • setViewDirection

      public void setViewDirection(String direction)
      Sets the direction of the monster
      Parameters:
      direction - the new direction of the monster

    • getViewSpriteNumber

      public int getViewSpriteNumber()
      Gets the number of the sprite to use to draw the monster
      Returns:
      the integer for the sprite number

    • setViewSpriteNumber

      public void setViewSpriteNumber(int spriteNumber)
      Sets the number of the sprite to use to draw the monster
      Parameters:
      spriteNumber - the number of the sprite to use

    • getViewInvincible

      public boolean getViewInvincible()
      Gets the invincibility state of the monster to modify how it's drawn
      Returns:
      true if the player has to be drawn in its invincibility state false otherwise

    • setViewInvincible

      public void setViewInvincible(boolean viewInvincible)
      Sets the invincibility state of the monster to modify how it's drawn
      Parameters:
      viewInvincible - the new invincibility state

    • getMonstersView

      public static MonsterView[] getMonstersView()
      Gets the array containing all the monsters to draw on the screen
      Returns:
      the array with the information about the monsters for the view

    • addMonstersView

      public static void addMonstersView(String name, int x, int y, int i)
      Adds a new monster in the array
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the monster
      x - the x-coordinate
      y - the y-coordinate
      i - the index in the array I want to add the monster

    • addMonstersView

      public static void addMonstersView(String name, int x, int y, int i, boolean isVisible)
      Adds a new monster in the array
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the monster
      x - the x-coordinate
      y - the y-coordinate
      i - the index in the array I want to add the monster
      isVisible - if the monster can be drawn

    • updateMonstersView

      public static void updateMonstersView(int i, int x, int y, int spriteNumber)
      Updates the fields of the monster with the values passed by the controller
      Parameters:
      i - the index of the array
      x - the x-coordinate to draw the monster
      y - the y-coordinate to draw the monster
      spriteNumber - the sprite number to use

    • getIsVisible

      public boolean getIsVisible()
      Getter for the field isVisible
      Returns:
      true if the monster can be drawn, false otherwise
